content: Cold weather concreting has critical pitfalls even with proper procedures. Overuse of accelerators can cause flash set, reduced long-term strength, and increased shrinkage cracking. Calcium chloride accelerators corrode steel reinforcement - never use in reinforced concrete. Insufficient insulation leads to thermal gradients causing differential expansion and cracking. Removing protection too early causes freeze damage - concrete must reach 500 psi before first freeze. Hot water mixing can cause cement flash set - limit mixing water to 140°F. Rapid temperature changes after curing cause thermal shock. Inadequate moisture retention during cold-weather curing leads to plastic shrinkage. Field testing is essential - don't rely solely on lab mix designs which may not reflect现场 conditions.
source: ACI 306R-16 & NRMCA Cold Weather Concreting Guidelines
